beats.activity: Wakes up, showers, throws on clothes, grabs coffee at home while scrolling on phone.
beats.reasoning: Jesse sleeps in late and wakes around 9am on a Tuesday shift day. Full energy at start means he's rested and ready to move.

beats.activity: Skateboards to work through Capitol Hill streets.
beats.reasoning: Short neighborhood commute by skateboard fits Jesse's restless, impulsive vibe and avoids transit closures.

beats.activity: Clocks in at Victrola, prepping espresso machines, taking orders, making lattes.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift at the coffee shop; Jesse needs background noise and activity, which the café provides perfectly.

beats.activity: Takes a break, grabs a stool at a nearby sports bar to catch the USA vs Wales FIFA match on the big screen with other patrons.
beats.reasoning: The 11am FIFA match is a real event; Jesse is energetic and social, so he'd absolutely duck out to watch it live with a crowd rather than miss it or watch alone later.

beats.activity: Skateboards back to Victrola to finish his shift.
beats.reasoning: Quick return within Capitol Hill; Jesse is pumped from the match and eager to wrap up work.

beats.activity: Finishes shift, cleaning espresso machine, cashing out register, chatting with coworkers.
beats.reasoning: Part-time shift wraps up; Jesse is loyal to his coworkers and lingers a bit to help close out the lunch rush.

beats.activity: Skateboards to the Capitol Hill Branch Library to volunteer at youth center art program.
beats.reasoning: Jesse volunteers on weekends and afternoons; light rail closure doesn't affect skateboard routes. Sunny weather is perfect for outdoor transit.

beats.activity: Volunteers at youth center, teaching kids graffiti-style art techniques, sketching alongside them, good heart showing through.
beats.reasoning: Jesse's good heart emerges here; he's loyal and gives back to youth. The structured environment with kids also provides the background noise and activity he craves.

beats.activity: Grabs fast food at a drive-through, heads home with a burger and fries.
beats.reasoning: Jesse loves fast food; he's earned a break after volunteering and is heading home. Quick errand fits his impulsive, restless nature.

beats.activity: Skateboard ride back home through Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Short return home; sunny weather makes the ride pleasant. Jesse is winding down after a full day.

beats.activity: Eats dinner, throws on music loud, sketches graffiti-style art at home, texts friends, scrolls social media, draws into the evening.
beats.reasoning: Jesse needs background noise and creative outlets; drawing late into the evening fits his artistic aspirations and restless personality perfectly.

diary: Monday was solid, yo. Woke up lazy but got moving, skateboarded through the Hill to Victrola like always. The morning rush was pretty chill—made a bunch of lattes, nothing too crazy. Around 11 I caught the USA vs Wales match at Shorty's with some randoms. That was sick, everyone was hyped. Got back to finish my shift, then headed to the library to work with the kids on their art. Those little dudes were so into it, drawing all these wild pieces. Grabbed a burger after and came home to just vibe with my sketchbook. Put on some music, drew for hours. Feels good when the day flows like that, you know? Like everything's exactly where it needs to be.
thought_process: I was feeling pretty energized today, maybe because of the weather or just because it's June. The FIFA match was a nice break—I don't usually get to just sit and chill like that during work hours, so I took it. The light rail being down didn't even matter since I skateboard everywhere anyway. What really got me was the art program though. Teaching those kids actually reminded me why I'm doing this whole thing, like there's a point to it. By the time I got home I was buzzing creatively, so I just drew until late. No drama, no overthinking, just flow.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
